title: Worker pane semantic painter
goal: Make the Herdr worker activity pane answer what the worker is doing by rendering typed Pi event payloads instead of stream-envelope noise and chunk counts.
acceptance: The worker pane suppresses low-value envelope events such as message_start/message_end and turn_start/turn_end by default, while preserving underlying daemon-owned stdout.ndjson artifacts unchanged for inspection.

Assistant text deltas are coalesced into readable bounded output at a human rate rather than reported primarily as chunk counts; the pane shows the payload text Pi emitted through typed pi_contract events.

Reasoning/progress display renders only content present in typed pi_contract events, such as provider-emitted thinking_delta or equivalent summaries, labels it as reasoning/progress, and never reconstructs hidden chain-of-thought from timing, token counts, unknown fields, or private provider data.

Tool activity lines include bounded identity and outcome where the typed events provide them, for example tool name, command/file/path summary, running/ok/failed state, and duration when available. Sensitive or long arguments are redacted/truncated for display without altering artifacts.

Recorded ndjson golden fixtures cover the observed screenshot warts: envelope events absent, tool identity present, assistant text visible, reasoning/progress labelled only when present in typed events, unknown events tolerated, and deltas coalesced.

The painter remains display-only and read-only: it never reads Herdr scrollback or pane text, never accepts operator answers/approvals/result JSON, and never changes worker correctness, result parsing, verification, merge, or handoff behavior.
Renderer failures remain non-fatal visibility incidents or pane warnings; killing the painter does not alter the daemon-owned wrapper/result path introduced by HERDR-04.
must_ask_if: The implementation would expose, infer, reconstruct, or label hidden chain-of-thought that is not present as typed Pi JSON event content.
The implementation would parse Pi wire-format event names or fields outside src/pi_contract.rs except in tests/fixtures.
The implementation would read Herdr pane text, terminal scrollback, or Herdr agent-status metadata as worker evidence.
The implementation would make painter output an input to worker result parsing, acceptance, verification, merge, or handoff.
The implementation would type into worker panes, auto-answer operator questions, or accept pasted/manual result JSON.
The implementation would remove the raw stdout.ndjson/stderr/status/result artifacts or make them less complete.
